steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has two contrasting halves: historians have solved 'Publius,' but pen names like 'Democritus' and 'Obadiah Spriggins' remain unsolved. The blank must signal that contrast — something like 'yet' or 'however.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'indeed,': Reinforces or emphasizes the preceding point — implies the unsolved names are a continuation, not a contrast, to the Publius success. ✗
  • Choice B — 'though,': Concessive transition that signals contrast — 'we solved Publius, though these others remain elusive.' ✓
  • Choice C — 'likewise,': Signals similarity — would wrongly suggest these names were also solved. ✗
  • Choice D — 'for example,': Signals an illustration of the previous point — would make the unsolved names examples of solved cases, which is the opposite of the meaning. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
